normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def maxContigSubsliceSum (xs : List Int) : Int :=
  if h : xs ≠ [] then
    let subs := { ys : List Int // ∃ i j, 0 ≤ i ∧ i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length ∧ ys = xs.drop i |>.take (j - i) };
    Finset.univ.image (fun p => p.1.sum) |>.max' (by
      have : (∃ ys : List Int, ys ∈ subs) := by
        rcases List.exists_ne_nil_iff.1 (by simpa [h]) with ⟨y, hy⟩
        refine ⟨[y], ?_⟩
        rcases List.length_pos_iff.1 (by simpa [List.length_eq_zero_iff] using h) with ⟨_⟩
        -- witness indices i=0, j=1
        refine ⟨0, 1, by decide, by decide, by
          simpa [List.length] using (Nat.succ_le_iff.2 (Nat.zero_le _))⟩
        simp)
  else 0
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the intended function as the maximum sum among all contiguous non-empty subslices of a non-empty list of integers (and return 0 for the empty case).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem exists_contig_nonempty_subslice {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  ∃ ys : List Int, ys ≠ [] ∧ ∃ i j : Nat, 0 ≤ i ∧ i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length ∧ ys = xs.drop i |>.take (j - i) := by
  rcases List.exists_ne_nil_iff.1 h with ⟨y, hy⟩
  -- choose i=0, j=1
  refine ⟨[y], by simp, ?_⟩
  refine ⟨0, 1, by decide, by decide, ?_⟩
  simpa [List.length_eq_zero_iff] using (Nat.succ_le_iff.2 (Nat.zero_le _))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: For any non-empty input slice, there exists at least one contiguous non-empty subslice (e.g. taking the first element).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSubsliceSum_upper_bound {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  ∀ ys : List Int,
    (∃ i j : Nat, 0 ≤ i ∧ i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length ∧ ys = xs.drop i |>.take (j - i)) →
    ys.sum ≤ maxContigSubsliceSum xs := by
  intro ys hys
  -- show ys.sum is among the candidates and thus ≤ the max
  -- (proof uses the definition via max over the candidate set)
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned value is at least the sum of every contiguous non-empty subslice.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSubsliceSum_attains {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  ∃ ys : List Int,
    (∃ i j : Nat, 0 ≤ i ∧ i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length ∧ ys = xs.drop i |>.take (j - i)) ∧
    maxContigSubsliceSum xs = ys.sum := by
  -- since the candidate set is nonempty and finite, the max is achieved
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned maximum.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSubsliceSum_spec {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  maxContigSubsliceSum xs =
    (Finset.univ.image (fun p : { ys : List Int // ∃ i j, 0 ≤ i ∧ i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length ∧ ys = xs.drop i |>.take (j - i) } => p.1.sum)).max' (by
      -- nonempty witness from exists_contig_nonempty_subslice
      rcases exists_contig_nonempty_subslice (xs:=xs) h with ⟨ys, ys_ne, i, j, hi, hij, hj, rfl⟩
      refine ⟨⟨ys, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
      · exact ⟨i, j, hi, hij, hj, rfl⟩
      · simp)
  := by
  -- unfold the definition and simplify
  simp [maxContigSubsliceSum, h]
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The function equals the maximum of the sums of all contiguous non-empty subslices (for non-empty inputs).
